name: paper
description: Academic paper style with numbered citations [1], [2], etc.

title.description: Paper title (same as the search query or custom title)

generated_at.description: Timestamp when the report was generated

body.description: Main body text with numbered citations like [1], [2]. Use markdown links for citations: [1](#ref-1), [2](#ref-2), etc. Each citation refers to a reference entry. IMPORTANT: Do NOT merge references from the same file - each citation should have its own unique reference number even if they are from the same document.

references.description: Array of references cited in the body

line_range.description: Line range (e.g., '42-50'). Auto-calculated when quote_start/quote_end are provided.

file_uri.description: Full file URI with line anchor (will be automatically encoded by MCP)

quote_preview.description: A short preview of the quoted content (1-2 lines, ~50-100 chars). Use the first meaningful line from the referenced section, truncate with '...' if too long.

quote_start.description: First ~30 characters copied verbatim from the CITED PORTION (not the entire chunk) for automatic line number resolution. When provided with quote_end, line_range is auto-calculated. The cited portion is the specific lines you are referencing in your text, not the whole chunk.

quote_end.description: Last ~30 characters copied verbatim from the CITED PORTION (not the entire chunk) for automatic line number resolution. When provided with quote_start, line_range is auto-calculated. The cited portion is the specific lines you are referencing in your text, not the whole chunk.

result_id.description: The chunk result ID from get_search_results (e.g., 'search_abc123_0'). Narrows the search range for more accurate line resolution.

example.title: Analysis of React Hooks Implementation
example.abstract: This paper examines the implementation of React hooks across the codebase. We identify key patterns and architectural decisions that enable functional components to manage state and side effects.
example.body: React hooks revolutionized functional component development [[1]](#ref-1). The useState hook provides state management capabilities [[2]](#ref-2), while useEffect handles side effects [[3]](#ref-3). These primitives enable complex component logic without class components.
